title: DDD Context Map SSOT

loop.observation: Context map child manifests are executable fragments used by this generated reader, but coverage previously counted them as loop-less standalone manifests.
loop.hypothesis: The context-map root can own the evidence loop while fragment manifests delegate to it, reducing exposed review surface without duplicating loop prose in every fragment.
loop.execute: Generate the context map reader, load every declared fragment, verify source and boundary anchors, and publish context-map evidence in public CI.
loop.evaluate: The verifier fails when a declared fragment is malformed, missing, stale, or no longer represented in the generated context map reader.
loop.retrospective: Context ownership remains a single generated SSOT surface while leaf fragments stay small data inputs with explicit loop provenance.

intro: This entrypoint owns the public riido-daemon bounded-context map after the repository split.
ownership_summary: Shared C1/C2/C3 contracts are consumed from riido-contracts.
SaaS C10 server behavior is owned by riido-control-plane.
Private deploy/evidence wiring is owned by riido-infra.
repository_boundary_rule: riido_daemon_private / riido-daemon-private is a retired historical source, not an active dependency, evidence store, or work target. Missing facts must be resolved in public riido-daemon SSOT or promoted to riido-contracts.
work_unit_boundary_rule: Every change starts from a Riido task. The task creation response branchName is the only allowed branch name for the work.

contexts.owner: github.com/teamswyg/riido-contracts/task; daemon mutates local rows through internal/taskdb only
github.com/teamswyg/riido-contracts/ir; daemon-side append/redaction boundary is internal/ir/ingest
github.com/teamswyg/riido-contracts/provider/capability; daemon provider adapters produce detected snapshots
internal/lock plus task DB sidecar leases
daemon-side polling/reporting adapter in internal/agentbridge/controlplane/saasplane; server behavior remains in riido-control-plane
